what_it_does: Records a design observation that opaque item IDs make graph nodes unreadable, and evaluates two strategies—frontmatter aliases/title-display plugin versus title-based mirror filenames—for improving legibility without breaking the wikilink mapping.
observations: Filenames use opaque IDs such as item-1492565a, so every graph node and link appears as a hash.
This creates friction for Tim, who reads meaning rather than IDs.
Using an aliases: frontmatter field or a title-display plugin lets notes display their title while the file remains item-XXX.md.
A mirror-with-title-filenames approach would produce the prettiest graph but would break the 1:1 board://item-XXX to [[item-XXX]] wikilink map.
Title-based filenames would require an id-to-title lookup table and would risk collisions and churn.
The note frames a tension between graph legibility and the zero-lookup property that keeps the system cheap.
standouts: The recommended approach is frontmatter aliases or a title-display plugin because it preserves the 1:1 wikilink map.
The central trade-off is described as legibility versus the zero-lookup property.
conventions: Frontmatter includes id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and created/updated/history.
Markdown body uses a numbered item heading format '8 · Display: ...'.
Claims are tagged with [Observed] and [Inferred] markers.
concerns: The mirror-with-title-filenames option is described as breaking a key architectural invariant; adopting it would require a formal id-to-title lookup and collision handling.
The recommendation is stated but not accompanied by a concrete decision record or implementation plan in this file.
notes: A design note arguing that opaque item IDs hurt graph readability and recommending frontmatter aliases or a title-display plugin so filenames and wikilinks stay stable.
questions: What does 'A2 only' refer to in the mirror-with-title-filenames option?
Which plugin or frontmatter field is currently used or planned for title display?
Who is Tim and what role does he play in evaluating the graph?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the opaque-ID display problem and compare solutions; the file does exactly that, so it matches.
intention_signals: It is trying to steer the system toward a display-layer fix rather than a filename change, so graph readability improves without sacrificing the cheap 1:1 ID-to-link mapping.
novelty: It explicitly frames the display problem as a conflict between human-readable graph labels and a zero-lookup wikilink invariant, and rejects title-based filenames on that basis.
contribution: It contributes a concrete recommendation for improving graph readability while preserving the existing ID-based file and link scheme, plus a clear articulation of the trade-off if title filenames are introduced.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item documenting that opaque item-ID filenames make graph nodes unreadable, recommending frontmatter aliases or a title-display plugin to show titles without changing filenames, and warning that title-based mirror filenames would break the 1:1 wikilink map.
intention_for_embedding: To decide how to make the item graph human-readable without losing the zero-lookup wikilink identity between board://item-XXX IDs and [[item-XXX]] links.
